name: Organization Name Consistent
description: Cross-references the brand name across Organization schema, og:site_name, title tag, and footer text. Inconsistent naming fragments entity recognition — Google and AI systems may treat name variants as different entities.

criteria.pass: Brand name is identical in: Organization schema name, og:site_name, title tag brand portion, footer copyright/brand text
criteria.warn: 1 source differs slightly (e.g., "TRYPS" vs "TRYPS App" vs "Tryps")
criteria.fail: 2+ sources differ, or significantly different names across sources

fix_template: Standardize brand name across all on-page references. Pick one canonical name and use it everywhere.
sources: Entity consistency helps AI systems unify brand identity. Inconsistent naming = fragmented entity recognition. Google and AI systems may treat "TRYPS" and "TRYPS App" as different entities.

name: Logo Consistent
description: Checks that the logo referenced in Organization schema matches og:image or favicon references. Visual brand consistency reinforces entity identity — a minor but easy-to-fix signal.

criteria.pass: Logo/image referenced in Organization schema matches og:image or favicon references
criteria.warn: Different images across schema and OG
criteria.fail: No logo in schema, or completely different images across sources

fix_template: Use the same logo URL in Organization schema logo field and og:image
sources: Visual brand consistency reinforces entity identity. Minor signal but easy to fix.

name: URL/Domain Consistent
description: Verifies canonical URL, schema @id, and og:url all reference the same domain and path. Domain inconsistency fragments siteAuthority — multiple domains without canonical consolidation split signals.

criteria.pass: Canonical URL, schema @id, og:url all reference the same domain and path
criteria.fail: Different domains or paths across these references (e.g., trypsagent.com in one, jointryps.com in another)

fix_template: Standardize on one domain. Set canonical. Ensure schema @id and og:url match canonical URL.
sources: Domain inconsistency fragments siteAuthority (confirmed in API leak). Multiple domains without proper canonical consolidation split signals.

name: sameAs URLs Resolve
description: Fetches every sameAs URL in the Organization schema and verifies it returns 200 and references the brand. Dead sameAs links signal neglected entity management and weaken trust when AI systems follow them to verify identity.

criteria.pass: All sameAs URLs in Organization schema return 200 status and contain brand references
criteria.warn: Some sameAs URLs resolve but don't clearly link back to the brand
criteria.fail: sameAs URLs return 404, redirect to unrelated pages, or don't resolve

fix_template: Remove dead sameAs URLs. Replace with active, verified profiles. Ensure each profile clearly references the brand.
sources: Dead sameAs links signal neglected entity management. AI systems that follow sameAs to verify identity will find dead ends — weakening trust.
notes: Verification requires fetching external profile URLs (outbound fetches beyond the audited page).

name: GBP Presence & Completeness
description: Looks for the business in the Google local pack observed for its tracked queries and checks the newest matching entry for the core listing fields: title, rating, votes, phone, url. A missing or incomplete Google Business Profile suppresses local visibility on every query that shows a pack.
method_detail: Pure DB read over stored serp_snapshots local-pack entries (gm/intel/local_presence.py); never reaches the classifier

criteria.pass: Your newest local-pack entry carries title, rating, votes, phone, and url
criteria.warn: You appear in a local pack but at least one core field (title/rating/votes/phone/url) is absent from the newest entry
criteria.fail: Local packs were observed for tracked queries but you appear in none of them
criteria.inconclusive: No local pack observed for any tracked query (or only legacy snapshots without entry data)

fix_template: Claim and complete the Google Business Profile: verify the listing, then fill in the business name, phone number, and website URL so the local-pack entry is complete. Operator-executed on the client's own listing.
sources: Minted in Phase D3 (growthmonk local-presence family, docs/phase-d3-contracts.md) — not from the aeo-seo-auditor ruleset. Local-pack presence is measured from SERP snapshots the platform already buys weekly; an unobserved pack is reported honestly as inconclusive, never guessed.
notes: Diagnostic only. Graded deterministically from stored local-pack sightings; requires DB/site context — 'na' on pre-publish drafts and on competitor_reference/comparison audits (client-site diagnostic).

name: NAP Consistency Across Sightings
description: Compares your local-pack entries across every tracked-query sighting: business name (title), phone, and domain must present one identity. Conflicting name/phone data, or a listing that points at a foreign domain, fragments the entity and erodes local ranking trust.
method_detail: Cross-ref of title/phone/domain across stored local-pack sightings (gm/intel/local_presence.py); never reaches the classifier

criteria.pass: One folded name+phone identity across all attributable sightings, and the listing domain matches the site's domain_norm
criteria.warn: Minor name/phone variants that differ by case or whitespace only
criteria.fail: Conflicting phone numbers or business names across sightings, or the listing points at a foreign domain
criteria.inconclusive: Fewer than 2 attributable local-pack sightings in the window

fix_template: Standardize the business name, phone number, and website URL on the Google Business Profile and every directory listing to one canonical NAP identity. Operator-executed on the client's own listings.
sources: Minted in Phase D3 (growthmonk local-presence family, docs/phase-d3-contracts.md) — not from the aeo-seo-auditor ruleset. NAP consistency is the local analogue of J-01: name/phone/domain variants fragment entity recognition across Google's local stack.
notes: Diagnostic only. Graded deterministically from stored local-pack sightings; requires DB/site context — 'na' on pre-publish drafts and on competitor_reference/comparison audits (client-site diagnostic).

name: Review Signal vs Local Pack
description: Compares your rating and review count against the medians of the other entries in your newest local-pack sighting. A below-median review signal costs local-pack clicks even when you appear. Diagnostic only: the platform measures the signal and never generates, drafts, solicits, templates, or automates reviews.
method_detail: Median comparison over stored local-pack entries (gm/intel/local_presence.py); never reaches the classifier

criteria.pass: Rating >= the pack median rating AND votes >= the pack median votes
criteria.warn: Below the median rating or below the median votes
criteria.fail: Rating more than 0.5 below the pack median, or votes below half the pack median
criteria.inconclusive: Your rating/votes, or every competitor's, are unobserved in the newest sighting

fix_template: Raise the review signal only through the client's own legitimate in-person review-request process (e.g. staff asking satisfied customers at checkout). Never generate, purchase, template, or automate reviews — this check is diagnostic only.
sources: Minted in Phase D3 (growthmonk local-presence family, docs/phase-d3-contracts.md) — not from the aeo-seo-auditor ruleset. Review volume and rating relative to the pack neighbours drive local-pack click share; the platform only measures the gap (do-not-build list, docs/01-product.md §6).
notes: Diagnostic only — no fix-closer path consumes this check. Graded deterministically from stored local-pack sightings; requires DB/site context — 'na' on pre-publish drafts and on competitor_reference/comparison audits (client-site diagnostic).
